main_sections.title: Change of Heart
main_sections.prose_summary: Prabhat is a competitive boy who cannot bear losing. He participates only in games he is certain to win, and even then he plays with fierce seriousness. When a new student, Surya, joins his school, Prabhat is immediately drawn to challenge him at badminton — the sport he excels at. Surya turns out to be a gifted player who approaches the game with effortless joy: he laughs, cracks jokes, and remains relaxed throughout, unconcerned with the score. Realising he is about to lose, Prabhat secretly alters the scoreboard while Surya is distracted and claims victory. Despite winning, Prabhat cannot sleep that night. He is troubled not only by the guilt of having cheated but by the inexplicable sight of Surya's contentment in defeat. In the following days, Prabhat quietly observes Surya playing basketball — a sport at which Surya is frankly hopeless — yet Surya's delight in the game never dims. Through this sustained observation, Prabhat arrives at a quiet but transformative insight: a scoreboard is not necessary to enjoy a game. What matters is wholehearted participation and the pleasure of the moment. Gradually, Prabhat himself begins to change. He joins games like hide-and-seek with genuine enthusiasm, starts joking during badminton, and earns from his classmates the very compliment that once seemed beyond him — that he has great sporting spirit. The story traces a moral arc from compulsive winning to the discovery of joyful, honest engagement, with Surya serving as a wordless model of what sporting spirit truly means.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us read + Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us do these activities before we read. I. Which games or sports do you play? II. Read the questions given below. Talk about them and make a list: 1. Which games or sports do you play for fun? 2. Which games or sports do you play for competition? III. How do you feel when you win or lose? IV. Work in pairs. Tick the correct meaning of the following.

question_bank.stem: Which games or sports do you play?
Which games or sports do you play for fun?
Which games or sports do you play for competition?
How do you feel when you win or lose?
Tick the correct meaning of the following: 1. friendly matches (i) matches played to win a trophy (ii) matches played without competition
options: matches played to win a trophy

question_bank.stem: Tick the correct meaning: opponent (i) someone who you compete with (ii) someone you want to be a partner with
options: someone you want to be a partner with

question_bank.stem: Tick the correct meaning: tally of marks (i) final marks on a report card (ii) record of points in a game/match
options: final marks on a report card
record of points in a game/match

question_bank.stem: Tick the correct meaning: sporting spirit (i) following all the rules in a game (ii) being fair and respecting each player in a game
options: following all the rules in a game
being fair and respecting each player in a game

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us discuss (after Part I of the story): Answer the following questions.

question_bank.stem: State whether the following is True or False: Prabhat would accept losing to anyone easily.
What was special about the last Friday of each month?
Who was responsible for maintaining the score for the match?
Do you think Prabhat wished to play another match with Surya? Why do you think so?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us discuss (after Part II of the story): Answer the following questions.

question_bank.stem: How did Surya feel about playing?
What was it about Surya that Prabhat observed?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us think and reflect — I. Read the lines given below and answer the questions.

question_bank.stem: If Prabhat sensed that he would lose a game, he would not play it. He would take part only when he was sure of winning, even if the game lasted only for a minute. You could not stop him from playing the kind of games he was really good at, like badminton. (i) Complete the sentence suitably: Prabhat would play a game only if ____________________. (ii) Which game was Prabhat good at playing? (iii) Select the most suitable option to complete the sentence: This behaviour of Prabhat tells us that he was ________________. A. good at the game  B. sure to win  C. afraid of losing

Surprisingly, Prabhat could not sleep well that night... (i) Select the one option that tells us about the way Prabhat was feeling that night. A. hurt  B. unhappy  C. afraid  D. focused (ii) Why does Prabhat find Surya's behaviour 'surprising'? (iii) Fill in the blank: We can say that Surya's reaction to not winning was ____________________ (similar to/as serious as/different from) Prabhat's.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us think and reflect — II. Answer the following questions.

question_bank.stem: How did Prabhat's attitude towards winning and losing affect his enjoyment of games in the beginning?
Why do you think Prabhat cheated in the beginning to win the badminton match against Surya?
How did Surya's attitude differ from Prabhat's?
Do you think Prabhat's approach to games was healthy? Why or why not?
Why do you think sporting spirit is important?
Explain how the title of the story, 'Change of Heart', is suitable.

listening.transcript_text: Good morning, everyone. I know you all are excited about the Friday friendly matches. This Friday we shall have three matches — one for chess, the next for kho-kho, and finally one for badminton. I have put up the kho-kho teams' name list on the sports' notice board. Since this is a team game, your class VI-B shall be playing against the VI-D kho-kho team. The kho-kho match refreshments are being sponsored by Xydium Sports Club. Now the places for the matches — chess will take place in the 1st floor activity room with Vani as the scorekeeper. Kho-kho will be played in the sports ground where I myself will be the referee. For badminton, we have chosen the multipurpose hall on the ground floor. For the chess match, it has been decided that this match shall be played between Sarita and Nupur. Two matches will be played during the regular games period on Friday but kho-kho will be played in the 2nd and 3rd period. Remember the kho-kho team names are up on the Sports' Notice Board. Good luck!

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to an announcement. As you listen, circle the pair that was chosen to play chess. (refer to page 128 for transcript)

question_bank.stem: Circle the pair that was chosen to play chess: Rima-Dhiraj / Gita-Hitesh / Sarita-Nupur

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will once again listen to the announcement. As you listen, fill in the notes with important details.

question_bank.stem: How many Friday matches will be played?
What was put up on the sports' notice board?
Who will sponsor the kho-kho team's refreshments?
Who will be the scorekeeper for chess?
Who will be the referee for kho-kho?
Which Friday periods are meant for kho-kho?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Look at the situations given below. In pairs, take turns to use the three magic words — 'please', 'thank you' and 'sorry' — for the situations given below. Use the prompts given in the table to learn how to thank and apologise properly.

question_bank.stem: You are Vani — You want to request your friends to take you in their team during games period.
You are Somiwon — You want to tell Hitesh to help you with the cleaning of the classroom cupboard.
You are Sumit — You accidently spilled water on Nupur's desk.
You are Hitesh — You forgot to get Simran's Art file to school.
You are Kriti — You spoke rudely to Nupur.
You are Mohan — You wish to thank Hitesh for helping you practice for the Inter-House speech competition.
You are Meher — You are grateful that Simran helped you understand a chapter taught at school when you were absent.
You are Rima — You are thankful to Gita for helping you collect the notebooks from the staff-room.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Prabhat managed to change the scoreboard while Surya was looking elsewhere. Yet, he did not feel so happy that night. Unable to sleep, he decided to write down his feelings in his diary. Given below is the page where he wrote his feelings that night. Complete it with the help of the phrases given in the box below: [I won the match / I do not feel happy about it / I will lose the match / I managed to change the scoreboard / I felt very happy / Everyone congratulated me after my win / Surya did not feel bad / He had fun playing with me]

question_bank.stem: Today, I defeated Surya in a badminton match but 1. __________________________. He was playing very well and was getting the points easily. I was sure that 2. _____________________. So, when everyone was laughing at a joke he cracked, 3. _____________________________. No one noticed the change and 4. ________________________________. What surprised me the most was that he said 5. _________________ and we should play again. How could he be so relaxed after losing a match? He should have been sad if not crying over his loss! Though 6. _______________________________, I did not enjoy as I usually do. It really disturbed me that 7. ____________________ about losing the match. It is still bothering me. Is it because I changed the score? I do not understand myself.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Make as many words as possible using consecutive letters, without changing the order of the letters, from the words in the boxes. One has been done for you.

question_bank.stem: BADMINTON — make words using consecutive letters (e.g., BAD, ADMIN, MINT, IN, TON, ON)
PHENOMENON — make words using consecutive letters
BASKETBALL — make words using consecutive letters
Now, you can choose words of your own and create such puzzles. Share them with your class and try solving others.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following sentences and circle the verbs.

question_bank.stem: Everyone was discussing their game and how Prabhat had played so well.
He had won the game but he did not feel as happy as he usually did.
What was more surprising that Surya had not felt bad about losing.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Arrange the circled verbs as the first action (old action) or second action (new action). One has been done for you. The verbs in Column A that refer to the first action or the old action are past perfect.

question_bank.stem: Arrange the verbs from sentence 2 (had won, did not feel) into Column A (First Action/Old) and Column B (Second Action/New).
question_bank.table:
S.No.|Column A — First Action (Old)|Column B — Second Action (New)
1.|had played|was discussing
2.| | 
3.| |

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Fill in the blanks with the past perfect form of verbs given within the brackets.

question_bank.stem: Prabhat __________ already __________ his homework before going to the badminton match. (finish)
Surya __________ basketball for years before joining the school's badminton team. (play)
By the time Prabhat arrived, Surya __________ already __________ several matches. (win)
The new student, Surya, __________ Prabhat before coming to the school. (never meet)
Before the games period, Prabhat __________ badminton extensively. (practise)

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following brief bio-sketches of sportspersons from India (Deepika Kumari and Neeraj Chopra). Visit the given link to know more about other Indian sportspersons and Arjuna Awardees.

question_bank.stem: Read the bio-sketches of Deepika Kumari and Neeraj Chopra. Discuss what qualities helped them succeed despite their humble beginnings.
Visit the Arjuna Awardees list (https://yas.nic.in/...) and find out about other Indian sportspersons.
